questions.question: 5 (a) Nickel(II) iodide crystals are hydrated. A sample of hydrated nickel(II) iodide crystals has the following composition by mass: Ni, 14.01%; I, 60.33%; H, 2.85%; O, 22.81%. Calculate the empirical formula of the hydrated nickel( II) iodide crystals. empirical formula = .............................. [2] (b) Molten nickel(II) iodide can be electrolysed using the apparatus show

parts.text: 5 (a) Nickel(II) iodide crystals are hydrated. A sample of hydrated nickel(II) iodide crystals has the following composition by mass: Ni, 14.01%; I, 60.33%; H, 2.85%; O, 22.81%. Calculate the empirical formula of the hydrated nickel( II) iodide crystals. empirical formula = .............................. parts.answer: 14.01/59 : 60.33/127 : 2.85/1 : 22.81/16 OR 0.237 : 0.475 : 2.85 : 1.43 Ni I H O (positive and negative) ions iodine Ni + + 2e − → Ni OR I − → I + 2 e − copper formed/copper deposited copper removed or copper lost or copper forms ions three from: (apparatus A ): solution becomes paler/fades in A (apparatus B ): solution stays the same colour in B (explanation): copper ions removed (but not added) copper ions not replaced in A OR copper ions both removed and added (at the same rate) copper ions are being replaced (continually) 0620/43 May/June 2018 0.12 4.14 Ca(C l O ) → CaC l + 3O 1 mark for O as product 1 mark for the rest correct and balanced → C l O − + H O +
